title: crepis lampsanoides
intro: Crepis lampsanoides, also known as Lampsan Crepis or Lampsan Hawk's-beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and aiding in the management of respiratory problems. Crepis lampsanoides can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for herbal remedies, and it is also used in some traditional folk medicine practices.
